steps.title: Trace the borrowing path
steps.content: Both examples show an Indigenous-language word entering English through Spanish. Coyote came from Nahuatl to Spanish to English, and condor came from Quechua to Spanish to English.

steps.title: Remove unsupported language claims
steps.content: Choice B compares total vocabulary contributions, which the passage does not measure. Choice C says Spanish contributed words to Indigenous languages, the opposite direction. Choice D claims equal numbers of Nahuatl and Quechua borrowings, which is never discussed. The answer is A.
